title: Megastachya obtusa
taxonomy: Megastachya obtusa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Megastachya, which is a group of grasses native to tropical Africa. The family Poaceae, also known as Gramineae, is a large and diverse group of grasses found in many parts of the world. Megastachya obtusa is further classified as a monocot, which means it has one cotyledon in its seedling stage. This classification is based on the plant's anatomy and reproductive structures.

intro: Megastachya obtusa, also known as "Cape grass", is a perennial grass native to South Africa, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and infections. The bioactive constituents of Megastachya obtusa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
